title: Conostegia icosandra
taxonomy: Conostegia icosandra is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This family is commonly known as the "melastome family" and consists of approximately 150 genera and 5,000 species. Conostegia icosandra is a member of the subfamily Melastomatoideae, which is the largest subfamily within the Melastomataceae family. The subfamily is characterized by a combination of unique morphological and anatomical features. Conostegia icosandra's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the family.

intro: Conostegia icosandra, also known as the Icogonia or the False Gardenia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. The plant is traditionally used in medicine due to its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to help alleviate various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Conostegia icosandra include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Conostegia icosandra is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often consumed to treat infections and promote overall well-being.
